title: Spring Onion and Ginger Sauce
summary: A fragrant, punchy sauce packed with fresh ginger and spring onion. Perfect as a dip, drizzle, or marinade, this Chinese-inspired condiment adds bold flavour to any dish.

method.description: Finely slice the spring onions and mince the fresh ginger. Add the finely sliced spring onions and minced ginger to a heatproof bowl.
Stir in the salt, MSG (if using), and sugar.
Heat the vegetable oil in a small pan over high heat until smoking.
Carefully pour the hot oil over the spring onion mixture, allowing it to sizzle and release its aroma.
Stir well and let sit for at least 5 minutes before serving.

tips: Storage: Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 5 days. Stir before using.
Substitutions: Swap vegetable oil for sesame oil for a nuttier flavour. Omit MSG if preferred.Serving Ideas: Drizzle over rice, noodles, grilled meats, or dumplings for an instant flavour boost.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
